topic: Responding to the Lifestyle Problems of Civilisational Development
quiz_title: AL — Introduction - Civilisational Development and Lifestyle [EN]
description: The impact of civilisational development on lifestyle, the emergence of physical inactivity, and fitness as a response.

questions.text: What most characterises the effect of civilisational development on everyday physical activity?
questions.explanation: As a result of technological progress, a significant portion of human labour became mechanised, leading to a reduction in everyday physical activity.

options.text: A continuous increase in physical activity
A significant decrease in physical activity
Physical activity appearing exclusively in sport

questions.text: Modern technological development increased everyday physical exertion.
questions.explanation: False, because technological development reduced the necessity of physical labour.

questions.text: What led to the emergence of a physically inactive lifestyle?
questions.explanation: The spread of mechanisation and sedentary work reduced daily physical activity.

options.text: An increase in opportunities for sport
The spread of mechanisation and sedentary work

questions.text: A physically inactive lifestyle can lead to health problems in the long term.
questions.explanation: True, because the absence of physical activity increases the risk of chronic disease.

questions.text: What problem does the fitness movement respond to?
questions.explanation: The fitness movement is a response to the negative effects of a physically inactive lifestyle.

options.text: The consequences of a physically inactive lifestyle

questions.text: Which statement best describes the role of recreation in modern society?
questions.explanation: Recreation represents intentional movement that helps maintain health alongside a physically inactive lifestyle.

questions.text: For modern people, movement is no longer an automatic part of everyday life.
questions.explanation: True, because a significant portion of physical activity has dropped out of the daily routine.

questions.text: Why has intentional exercise become necessary in modern life?
questions.explanation: Because natural physical exertion is absent from everyday life.

options.text: Because there is more leisure time
Because it has been made compulsory
Because the importance of sport has declined

questions.text: One consequence of civilisational development is the automatic replacement of physical activity.
questions.explanation: False, because movement is not automatically replaced — it must be deliberately incorporated.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a typical consequence of a physically inactive lifestyle?
questions.explanation: A physically inactive lifestyle does not increase but rather decreases physical performance capacity.

questions.text: What is one fundamental contradiction of modern lifestyle?
questions.explanation: Modern life reduces physical activity while simultaneously increasing the need for it.

options.text: Little movement, yet greater need for it

questions.text: The goal of recreational physical activities is to maximise performance.
questions.explanation: False, because the primary goal of recreation is health preservation and regeneration.

questions.text: Which factor contributed most to the decline in physical activity?
questions.explanation: Technological progress and mechanisation brought about the greatest change.

questions.text: What is one of the most important roles of the concept of fitness in modern society?
questions.explanation: Fitness helps counteract the negative effects of a physically inactive lifestyle.
